channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law resigns from Trump administration intelligence roles
item.description: Amaryllis Fox Kennedy, daughter-in-law of U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is stepping down from her two senior intelligence roles in the Trump administration. Kennedy h...

item.title: Protesters in Makueni Briefly Take Police Firearm During Fuel Price Demonstrations
item.description: On Monday, May 18, 2026, demonstrations against rising fuel prices in Makueni County turned tense when protesters allegedly seized a police firearm. A viral video showed a man wearing a yellow top and...

item.title: Historical Portraits of Women Involved in New York's Mafia
item.description: Throughout much of the 20th century, New York City’s organized crime scene was dominated by infamous figures such as Lucky Luciano, Bugsy Siegel, Meyer Lansky, and Frank Costello. While the men of the...

item.title: Rapid Growth of Global Drone Industry Expected to Reach $160bn by 2034
item.description: Former Director-General of the Nigeria Civil Aviation Authority, Capt Musa Nuhu, don yan say the global drone market (UAV) fit grow reach over $160 billion by 2034. He talk this one for a paper wey e ...

item.title: NBA Anti-Corruption Chair Urges Lawyers to Uphold Integrity
item.description: The National Chairman of the NBA Anti-Corruption Committee, Prof. Babafemi A. Badejo, don raise alarm say corruption don dey normal for Nigeria. E call on lawyers across the country to take lead in br...

item.title: PDP faction clears Jonathan to contest 2027 presidency
item.description: The Kabiru Turaki-led faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) don grant former President Goodluck Jonathan waiver to fit contest the 2027 presidential election. According to Babangida Aliyu, for...

item.title: Indian PM Narendra Modi Avoids Press Questions During Norway Visit
item.description: During his official visit to Norway as part of a six-day, five-nation tour, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi faced criticism for refusing to take questions from the press. The incident occurred at ...

item.title: Anjelica Huston: A Legendary Career in Hollywood
item.description: Anjelica Huston, born into Hollywood royalty as the daughter of famed director John Huston, has established herself as one of the most compelling actresses of her generation. Known for her aristocrati...

item.title: A Retrospective on Robert Duvall’s Iconic 1970s Film Roles
item.description: Robert Duvall, one of Hollywood’s most respected actors, passed away peacefully at his home in Middleburg, Virginia, on February 15, 2026, at the age of 95. Known for his exceptional talent in both le...

item.title: Review: Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Combines Lego Humor with Arkham-Inspired Gameplay
item.description: Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is TT Games’ latest entry into the Lego Batman universe, blending RPG, brawler, and classic Lego platforming elements into a fun, family-friendly adventure. The ...

item.title: Taiwan Candidate Sparks Debate Over Anti-India Billboard During Local Election Campaign
item.description: In Kaohsiung, Taiwan, Lee Hung-yi, a ward chief of Gangming borough and independent candidate for the upcoming city council elections, has attracted widespread attention for putting up a billboard per...

item.title: Commonwealth Short Story Prize Winners Under Scrutiny for Possible AI Use
item.description: The 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize winners have come under intense scrutiny after allegations emerged that several of the winning stories may have been generated, wholly or partially, using artif...

item.title: Arsenal Wins Premier League After 22-Year Wait
item.description: Arsenal don finally clinch the 2025/26 Premier League title after waiting 22 years for another top-flight crown. Dem wahala for Manchester City side as them draw against Bournemouth na wetin make Mike...

item.title: JD Vance deflects questions on Trump's stock trading amid ethics concerns
item.description: Vice President JD Vance faced questions on Tuesday regarding President Donald Trump's stock trading practices, which were highlighted in recent financial disclosures. The disclosures revealed that Tru...

item.title: MK Party appoints Sifiso Mahlangu as new spokesperson, removes Nhlamulo Ndhlela
item.description: The uMkhonto weSizwe Party (MK Party) has announced the immediate removal of Nhlamulo Ndhlela as its national spokesperson and as a member of parliament. This decision was confirmed during a media bri...

item.title: Republican opposition grows against $1B White House ballroom funding
item.description: Funding for President Donald Trump's planned White House ballroom, which is part of a broader East Wing modernization project, is facing increasing opposition from within the Republican Party. While t...

item.title: Global Health Authorities Assess Risks of Ebola and Hantavirus Outbreaks
item.description: Recent developments in global health have sparked renewed concerns about potential pandemics. A rare hantavirus has been reported on a cruise ship, resulting in three fatalities and several infections...

item.title: Edmund Adler: Capturing the Innocence of Childhood in Art
item.description: Edmund Adler (1876–1965) was an Austrian genre painter celebrated for his tender and detailed depictions of childhood and rural life. Trained at the Vienna Academy, Adler had a remarkable ability to p...

item.title: Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe signals openness to negotiate industrial carbon tax with federal government
item.description: Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe has indicated that his province is willing to engage in discussions with the federal government to find a workable approach to an industrial carbon tax. Following a rece...

item.title: Postponement of Judgment in Usindiso Building Fire Case Leaves Families Waiting
item.description: The highly anticipated judgment in the Usindiso building fire case has been postponed indefinitely by the Palm Ridge Magistrate’s Court, prolonging closure for families affected by one of South Africa...
